source.title: Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage, Fire, Crane/Other Lifting

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Mississippi Canyon Block 547

actions_taken: All 36 personnel reported to muster stations
Control Room Operator identified fire location
Lead Operator/On-Scene Commander applied water with water monitor
Fire Team donned Firefighter Turnout Gear and applied water via hose reel
Fire Team opened air compressor door to verify fire extinguishment
Fire Team removed and soaked air filters with water
Generator enclosure CO2 fire suppression system activated automatically
event.summary: On 24 December 2023 at 0743 hours, a fire occurred on a rental air compressor being used for painting and blasting operations on the FPU 'Who Dat' in MC 547 A, approximately 39 miles offshore in 3280 feet of water. The fire was caused by failure of a 90° elbow on the oil separator piping, which allowed oil to spray into the enclosure and ignite on hot surfaces. The platform's fire detection system and Emergency Shut Down system functioned as designed. The fire was safely extinguished by the platform's Fire Team using water. No injuries, environmental impact, or platform damage occurred. Damage was limited to the rental air compressor and minor charring of adjacent generator enclosure wiring. Cost of repairs/replacement: $31,969.09.

hazards.name: Hot surfaces and ignition sources within air compressor enclosure

threats.name: Oil separator piping failure at 90° elbow
Oil spray into air compressor enclosure
Oil contact with hot surfaces causing ignition

consequences.name: Damage to adjacent generator enclosure wiring
Platform Emergency Shut Down and production loss

controls.name: Temperature Safety Element (TSE) Zone 1A detection system
controls.barrier_role: Early detection of abnormal temperature conditions
controls.lod_basis: Automatic detection system that triggers ESD

supporting_text: A Control Room Operator (CRO) saw on the Human Machine Interface (HMI) screen that the platform had tripped on a Temperature Safety Element (TSE) for Zone 1
the rental air compressor TSE system (Zone 1A TSE) tripped four seconds after the facility's Generator Fire-Eye Detection system tripped

controls.name: Emergency Shut Down (ESD) system
controls.barrier_role: Automatic shutdown of facility operations upon fire detection
controls.lod_basis: Automatic system triggered by fire detection

supporting_text: The ESD activation disabled the main power supply to the facility and sounded the alarm for all 36 POB to report to their assigned muster station

controls.name: Generator Fire-Eye Detection system
controls.barrier_role: Automatic detection of fire in generator enclosure

supporting_text: the facility's Generator Fire-Eye Detection system tripped
The fire detection and CO2 fire suppression system within the generator enclosure activated as designed to extinguish any fire within the generator enclosure

controls.name: CO2 fire suppression system in generator enclosure
controls.barrier_role: Automatic fire suppression within generator enclosure
controls.lod_basis: Automatic suppression system triggered by fire detection

supporting_text: The fire detection and CO2 fire suppression system within the generator enclosure activated as designed to extinguish any fire within the generator enclosure
automatically flooding the inside of the generator enclosure (ZZZ-9000) with CO2 to extinguish any fire and protect the generator

controls.name: Zone 1 deluge system
controls.lod_basis: Automatic deluge system for zone fire suppression

supporting_text: The deluge system in Zone 1 was also activated

controls.name: Manual fire suppression by Lead Operator/On-Scene Commander
controls.barrier_role: Manual application of water to fire source

supporting_text: the Lead Operator/On-Scene Commander (LO/OSC), reported to the scene and noticed that the fire was coming from the rental air compressor
The LO/OSC then notified the control room of the fire's location and began applying water with a nearby water monitor

controls.name: Fire Team response with Firefighter Turnout Gear
controls.barrier_role: Trained personnel with protective equipment for fire suppression
controls.lod_basis: Trained emergency response team with appropriate PPE

supporting_text: a Fire Team (FT) consisting of three personnel donned their appropriate Firefighter Turnout Gear and reported to the Offshore Installation Manager/Incident Commander (OIM/IC)
the OIM/IC led the FT to the LO/OSC, who then directed the FT to grab a hose reel and begin applying water to the rental air compressor

controls.name: Fire watch monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Post-suppression monitoring to ensure fire does not reignite

supporting_text: At approximately 0754 hours, the FT started a 1-hour fire watch to monitor the scene and ensure the fire was completely extinguished

controls.name: Personnel muster and accountability system
controls.barrier_role: Ensure all personnel are accounted for and in safe locations
controls.lod_basis: Mandatory muster station reporting upon ESD activation

supporting_text: sounded the alarm for all 36 POB to report to their assigned muster station
CRO2 to make the announcement over the platform's intercom system, 'This is a real event, there is a fire by the generator!'

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
